front: What is the formal statement of the Pigeonhole Principle?
back: If k is a positive integer and k+1 or more objects are placed into k boxes, then at least one box contains two or more of the objects.

front: What is the generalized or strong form of the Pigeonhole Principle?
back: If N objects are placed into k boxes, then there is at least one box containing at least ⌈N/k⌉ objects (the ceiling of N divided by k).

front: Provide a common example application of the Pigeonhole Principle.
back: In a group of 367 people, at least two people must share the same birthday, as there are only 366 possible birthdays (including February 29th).

front: What is the key insight the Pigeonhole Principle provides in combinatorics and proof writing?
back: It is a powerful tool for proving the existence of a certain arrangement or property (e.g., duplication, collision) without necessarily having to identify the specific instance, often used in existence proofs.

front: In the context of functions and relations, how can the Pigeonhole Principle be applied?
back: If A and B are finite sets and |A| > |B|, then a function f: A → B cannot be injective (one-to-one). At least two elements in A must map to the same element in B.
